methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $365M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Noveon Magnetics is a Series C-stage materials company founded by Scott Dunn and Peter Afiuny that has raised $365M to date. The relevant comp universe for this stage and sector is limited, with one identified private peer (Lyten) and no public comparables in the materials frontier-tech space that provide direct valuation anchors.

Given the single methodological approach available, a stage-progression framework was applied. Using total capital raised ($365M) as a proxy for enterprise value and applying a typical Series C-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 1.7x yields an implied valuation of $620M. This represents a disciplined extrapolation from observed capital progression patterns rather than market-derived multiples.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $527M (low) to $714M (high), with a median of $620M. The range reflects reasonable variance around the step-up multiplier methodology, but the tight comp set—only one private peer identified and no public equivalents—materially constrains confidence in this estimate. Key uncertainties include the absence of revenue or profitability data to cross-check valuation against fundamental metrics, the limited peer universe for benchmarking, and whether the 1.7x multiplier appropriately reflects current market conditions for frontier materials ventures. Additional diligence on comparable Series C outcomes in adjacent deep-tech sectors would strengthen conviction.
